title: bupleurum protractum subsp. savignonii
intro: Bupleurum protractum subsp. savignonii, also known as Savigni's hares ear, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bioactive compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being. Bupleurum protractum subsp. savignonii can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to promote health and prevent disease.
